sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.81 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite$p$-group admitting an automorphism$$of prime order$q$with$|C_G(a)| n$. If$p = q$, then does$G$have a nilpotent subgroup of class at most 2 and index bounded by a function of$n$? If$p q$, then does$G$have a nilpotent subgroup of class bounded by a function of$q$and index bounded by a function of$n$(and, possibly,$q$)? { B.\,Hartley } a) Not necessarily (E.\,I.\,Khukhro, Math. Note...
